sources.summary: New taskforce and £54 million fund will attract world-class researchers and their teams to the UK and comes ahead of the launch of government’s modern Industrial Strategy.

sources.label: Replacing animals in science strategy
sources.title: Replacing animals in science strategy
sources.summary: The strategy will help create, test, and adopt alternative methods to animal testing so we can work towards phasing out the use of animals in all but exceptional circumstances.

sources.title: Statement of Policy Intent: Early Access to Innovative Medical Devices
sources.summary: The MHRA's initial plans on an Early Access service, which will be developed further throughout 2025.

sources.label: MHRA and NICE aligned pathway early adopters
sources.title: MHRA and NICE invite early adopters to trial accelerated aligned pathway – six months ahead of schedule
sources.summary: Aligned pathway will streamline decisions on licensing and value for medicines, helping patients to benefit from them sooner.

sources.label: Integrated scientific advice guidance
sources.title: Medicines: Get integrated scientific advice from the MHRA and NICE
sources.summary: Using the Integrated Scientific Advice service from the Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) and National Institute for Health and Care Excellence (NICE).

sources.summary: 12-month roll-out begins today for the biggest regulatory shake up of clinical trials in 20 years

sources.label: HDRS chair appointment
sources.title: Visionary leader appointed for Health Data Research Service
sources.summary: Patients to benefit from faster life-saving treatments as new leader appointed for health data service.

sources.label: HDRS CEO appointment
sources.title: Healthcare innovator appointed to Health Data Research Service
sources.summary: Healthcare industry innovator Dr Melanie Ivarsson has been appointed as CEO of the Health Data Research Service (HDRS) to accelerate medical breakthroughs.

sources.label: Data Use and Access Act implementation update
sources.title: New data laws will make life easier for British people, cutting life admin, easing traffic and speeding up roadworks
sources.summary: British people will benefit from new laws which will make their day-to-day lives easier – from slashing grocery bills and cutting traffic jams to speeding up NHS diagnoses – as the government delivers on manifesto commitments.

sources.label: Prime Minister turbocharges medical research
sources.title: Prime Minister turbocharges medical research
sources.summary: Better and faster access to NHS data for researchers, with gold standard security and privacy measures.

sources.label: UK Biobank Research Analysis Platform
sources.title: Research Analysis Platform
sources.summary: Learn about the UK Biobank Research Analysis Platform (UKB-RAP), a secure, sophisticated, and user-friendly cloud-based tool.

sources.label: New UK Biobank data available
sources.title: Metabolomic, genetic, imaging data and more: new and updated UK Biobank data now available
sources.summary: The latest update to UK Biobank’s comprehensive dataset is available to approved researchers around the world via the Research Analysis Platform (UKB-RAP).

sources.label: Our Future Health opens to researchers
sources.title: World’s largest cohort study now open to researchers
sources.summary: Our Future Health, the world’s largest consented cohort study of its kind, is now open to researchers, as published in Nature Medicine

sources.label: The UK shows its support for Our Future Health
sources.title: The UK shows its support for Our Future Health
sources.summary: As the government announces new funding for our programme, we can reveal that over 2.5 million people have now volunteered from across the country

sources.label: Genomics England research data
sources.title: Data | Genomics England
sources.summary: Genomics England Research Environment has one of the largest genomic data sets enriched with clinical data. We enable scientists from academia and industry to make discoveries, laying the foundations for personalised medicine for rare conditions and cancer.

If you click 'Accept all cookies' we'll assume that you are happy to receive all cookies and you won't see this message again. sources.summary: This funding opportunity is for established researchers from across UK Research and Innovation (UKRI)’s remit, without a background in core artificial intelligence (AI) research, who want to build domain relevant AI capability and develop advanced AI approaches to tackle a specific research challenge in their chosen field.
